summary: Researched Express rate limiting patterns. Recommend per-IP tracking with Map, 429 + Retry-After header, configurable limits, and global 15-min interval reset.
keyFindings: Store count per IP in a Map
Global setInterval clears Map every 15 minutes
decisions: Global reset interval (not per-IP sliding window) per user spec
In-memory only, no Redis/external store needed
risks: In-memory Map doesn't scale across clustered processes

summary: Implemented rate limiter middleware factory with per-IP Map tracking, 429 responses with Retry-After, X-RateLimit headers, and 15-min global reset via setInterval.
keyFindings: interval.unref() prevents blocking Node process exit
Exposed resetAll() and stop() helpers for testing/cleanup
Adopted all Scout recommendations: req.ip, Retry-After, X-RateLimit headers
decisions: Used factory pattern over plain middleware for configurability
Used module-level Map shared across factory calls for simplicity
unref() on interval so it doesn't keep process alive
risks: Single shared Map means multiple factory calls share state

summary: Reviewed rate limiter middleware. APPROVE with 2 minor notes about shared module-level Map across factory calls. No security, correctness, or performance issues.
keyFindings: Factory pattern enables testability via resetAll/stop helpers
interval.unref() correctly prevents blocking process exit
Minor: shared Map means multiple factory calls share state
Minor: multiple factory calls create redundant intervals
decisions: APPROVE — no critical or major issues
Shared Map is acceptable for single-middleware scope
risks: Multi-call factory sharing state could confuse consumers in larger apps
